title: Procurement Specialist
description:
JOB DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

1. Procurement Workflow Redesign

  • Review existing procurement workflows across business units to identify inefficiencies, duplication, and control gaps.
  • Redesign and standardise end-to-end procurement processes, including requisitioning, sourcing, contracting, purchasing, and payment.
  • Develop process maps, standard operating procedures (SOPs), and control frameworks aligned with organisational policies and best practices.
  • Support change management activities, including stakeholder engagement, communications, and training.

2. Harmonisation of Material Codes

  • Review and rationalise existing material and service codes used across business units.
  • Develop and maintain a standardised material master structure, naming conventions, and classification rules.
  • Coordinate with business users to cleanse, consolidate, and migrate legacy material data.
  • Establish governance processes to ensure ongoing data accuracy and consistency.

3. ERP Centralised Procurement Module Support

  • Support the design, configuration, and testing of the centralised procurement module within the ERP system.
  • Translate business procurement requirements into functional specifications for system implementation.
  • Participate in system integration testing (SIT), user acceptance testing (UAT), and data migration activities.
  • Assist in preparing user guides, training materials, and post-go-live support documentation.
  • Participate in ERP system development and other related modules support.

4. Stakeholder & Project Support

  • Work closely with business units, digitalisation and finance teams, and external consultants to ensure alignment and timely delivery.
  • Track project milestones, risks, and issues related to procurement transformation initiatives.
  • Provide ad-hoc procurement analysis, reports, and support as required.

SKILLS REQUIREMENT

  • Strong understanding of end-to-end procurement processes and

controls.

  • Good communication and documentation skills.
  • Ability to work with cross-functional stakeholders and manage  
  • change.
  • Detail-oriented, structured, and able to manage multiple   

workstreams concurrently.

  • Experience in process mapping and documentation (e.g. BPMN,

               Visio).

  • Strong analytical and data management skills.

QUALIFICATIONS, KNOWLEDGE

  • Degree in Procurement, Supply Chain Management, Business, Finance, Information Systems, or a related discipline.
  • Professional certification (e.g. CIPS, CPSM, or equivalent) is an advantage.

YEARS OF RELEVANT WORK EXPERIENCE(S) REQUIRED

  • 3–7 years of experience in procurement, supply chain, or procurement transformation roles.
  • Hands-on experience in procurement process improvement or workflow redesign.
  • Experience in ERP implementation or enhancement projects (e.g. SAP, Oracle, Workday, Microsoft Dynamics).
  • Experience in material master data management or data standardisation is highly desirable.

postedCompany.description:
About Catholic Welfare Services Singapore

Catholic Welfare Services (CWS) was founded in 1959 on the spirit of the Good Shepherd to lead people to love and fulfillment, and the Spirit of the Good Samaritan to help those who have fallen along the way.

Over the years, CWS has grown to become an action arm of the Catholic Church in Singapore, alleviating poverty and distress among the lost sheep of society, irrespective of race or religion, by being their caring shepherd.

Our Services & Programmes

From its humble beginnings of providing food relief to the poor, CWS services and programmes now include:

  1. HQ Services
  2. Night Mission
  3. Heart@Work
  4. St Joseph's Home
  5. St Theresa's Home
  6. Villa Francis Home
  7. Gift of Joy
  8. St Vincent Home
  9.  CWS - BT YouthReach
postedCompany.name: Catholic Welfare Services, Singapore
